task: Create the src/middleware directory if it doesn’t exist, then add src/middleware/fortress.js exporting an Express middleware and Koa middleware that set X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, and (when FORTRESS_HSTS=true) Strict-Transport-Security, plus an Express Router with GET /status/fo returning 200 and JSON { status: "ok", fortress: true }; update src/server.js to app.use the fortress middleware early and mount its router, and add test/test_fortress.test.js using supertest to verify the /status/fo response and presence of all security headers.

subtasks.description: Create the middleware directory (if needed) and add src/middleware/fortress.js which exports (a) an Express middleware function that sets the required security headers and an Express Router with GET /status/fo returning JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, and (b) a Koa-compatible middleware function that sets the same headers and exposes the same status route behavior when mounted in Koa.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/middleware/fortress.js exists, exports at least: { expressMiddleware, expressRouter, koaMiddleware } (module.exports or export). File should not start a server on require and should be free of syntax errors.

changes.explanation: Adds a new middleware module that exports an Express middleware, an Express Router with GET /status/fo, and a Koa-compatible middleware. Both middleware variants set a common set of security headers and the status route returns the required JSON. The file avoids starting a server and guards requiring express so it fails gracefully if express isn't installed.

subtasks.description: Edit src/server.js to import the fortress module and (a) app.use the fortress Express middleware early in middleware chain (near top of app setup) and (b) mount the fortress expressRouter so GET /status/fo is reachable. Also ensure src/server.js exports the Express app (module.exports = app) and only starts listening when run directly (guarded by if (require.main === module)).
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/server.js loads without starting a server when required, exports the Express app, and the fortress middleware/router are required and mounted early. Requiring the file should return an object with app.use function.

subtasks.description: Add a Jest/SuperTest test at test/test_fortress.test.js that starts the exported app (require('./src/server.js')), then performs a GET /status/fo and asserts status 200, body { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, and that the response includes the headers: X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block (or exact header string), Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, and when environment FORTRESS_HSTS=true is set, Strict-Transport-Security header is present.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: jest test completes and passes: GET /status/fo returns 200 with JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true } and all the required security headers are present. When running the test with FORTRESS_HSTS=true in the environment, the Strict-Transport-Security header must be present.

plan_summary: Add a Fortress middleware module (Express + Koa) under src/middleware, wire it into the existing src/server.js so it's applied early and its router mounted, and add a SuperTest/Jest test to verify /status/fo response and security headers. Install test dev-deps before running tests.
risk_assessment: Medium -- editing src/server.js may break app startup if not done carefully; tests rely on server exporting app without starting to avoid port conflicts.
